subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Dark Energy: The Accelerating Universe
description: A quick, practical guide to Dark Energy: The Accelerating Universe.

hook.text: The universe isn't just expanding—it's speeding up. Something is pushing space apart, and nobody knows what.

buildup.text: In 1998, two teams studying distant supernovae expected to find the expansion slowing. They found the opposite.

discovery.text: The results showed expansion is accelerating. The unknown cause was named dark energy—it makes up 68% of the universe.

twist.text: Einstein's discarded cosmological constant fits the data perfectly. His 'biggest blunder' might have been correct all along.

climax.text: Perlmutter, Schmidt, and Riess won the 2011 Nobel Prize. Dark energy remains the biggest mystery in physics.

punchline.text: Sixty-eight percent of the universe. Completely mysterious. Pushing everything apart.

quiz.question: What was surprising about the 1998 supernova observations?
options: The universe's expansion is accelerating rather than slowing down
Supernovae were brighter than expected in distant galaxies
Dark matter was found to be repelling galaxies
